questions.scenario: Combat starting. You haven't applied Kardia yet.
questions.question: Who should receive Kardia?
questions.explanation: Kardia heals the target when you deal damage. Apply it to the tank - they take consistent damage and benefit most.

questions.scenario: Tank at 20% HP. You have Druochole and Taurochole available.
questions.question: Which is better for emergency healing?
questions.explanation: Taurochole heals AND provides 10% mitigation. For emergency healing on tank, the extra mitigation helps prevent further drops.

questions.scenario: Tank swap happening. New tank will take aggro.
questions.question: Should you swap Kardia?
questions.explanation: Kardia should be on whoever is actively tanking. Swap it to the new main tank so your damage heals them.

questions.scenario: You're spamming Dosis. Tank has Kardia.
questions.question: What's happening to the tank?
questions.explanation: Kardia heals the target every time you deal damage with a GCD. Your Dosis spam = constant tank healing. DPS = healing on SGE.

questions.scenario: DPS at 25% HP. Tank is fine. No Addersgall available.
questions.question: What's your best option?
questions.explanation: With no Addersgall, Diagnosis (GCD heal) is your option. Kardia on DPS temporarily could work but is clunky. Diagnosis gets them to safe HP.

questions.scenario: You have 3 Addersgall. A stack regenerates in 2 seconds.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: At 3 stacks with regen incoming, you'll overcap and waste a stack. Spend at least one before the regen.

questions.scenario: Raidwide coming. Party at full HP. Kerachole available.
questions.question: Should you use Kerachole?
questions.explanation: Kerachole provides 10% mitigation AND a HoT. Use it BEFORE the raidwide for mitigation value, then HoT heals the damage.

questions.scenario: Tankbuster incoming. Tank at 80% HP.
questions.question: What should you apply?
questions.explanation: Taurochole provides heal + 10% mitigation. Use BEFORE the buster so mitigation reduces the hit, then heal tops them off.

questions.scenario: Party at 50% HP after raidwide. Kerachole on CD.
questions.question: What should you use?
questions.explanation: Ixochole is instant party healing for 1 Addersgall. At 50% HP, it brings party to safety quickly. GCD heals are slower.

questions.scenario: Tank at 60% HP. You have 1 Addersgall. No major damage coming.
questions.question: Should you use Druochole?
questions.explanation: 60% HP with no incoming damage is safe. Your Kardia heals + natural regen handle this. Save the Addersgall for when it's needed.

questions.scenario: Tank taking heavy damage. Soteria available.
questions.question: What does Soteria do?
questions.explanation: Soteria boosts Kardia healing by 50% for 15 seconds. Use it during high tank damage phases to increase your passive healing.

questions.scenario: Heavy party-wide damage phase. Philosophia available.
questions.question: What's special about Philosophia?
questions.explanation: Philosophia makes your damage heal the ENTIRE party (like Kardia for everyone) for 20 seconds. Amazing for sustained AoE damage.

questions.scenario: Soteria available. Tank at 90% HP taking light damage.
questions.question: Should you use Soteria?
questions.explanation: Soteria is best during heavy tank damage. At 90% with light damage, your base Kardia is enough. Save Soteria for when you need the boost.

questions.scenario: Boss dies in 10 seconds. Philosophia still available.
questions.question: Should you use it?
questions.explanation: If there's any party damage in the final 10 seconds, Philosophia provides value. Don't hold cooldowns when the fight is ending.

questions.scenario: Soteria is active. You need to stop DPSing to heal with GCDs.
questions.question: How does this affect Kardia?
questions.explanation: Kardia only heals when you deal damage. If you stop DPSing to use GCD heals, Kardia (and Soteria's boost) stops working.

questions.scenario: You want to apply a shield to the tank.
questions.question: What's the correct sequence?
questions.explanation: Eukrasia modifies your next Diagnosis into E.Diagnosis (shield). Press Eukrasia first, then Diagnosis.

questions.scenario: E.Dosis (DoT) has 5 seconds remaining on boss.
questions.question: Should you refresh?
questions.explanation: Refresh DoTs at 3 seconds or less. At 5 seconds, refreshing wastes ticks. Wait a bit longer.

questions.scenario: Raidwide in 3 seconds. Party at full HP.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: E.Prognosis gives party-wide shields. Before raidwide, shields reduce incoming damage. Apply proactively.

questions.scenario: Tankbuster coming. Tank already has a shield from E.Diagnosis.
questions.question: Can you apply another E.Diagnosis shield?
questions.explanation: Shields from the same source don't stack. A new E.Diagnosis only refreshes if the new shield would be larger.

questions.scenario: You need to move and can only fit one instant GCD.
questions.question: What's the best choice?
questions.explanation: Phlegma is instant and strong damage. During movement when you can only fit one GCD, it's ideal. Save it for these moments.

questions.scenario: Raidwide just hit. Party at 60%. Physis available.
questions.question: Should you use Physis?
questions.explanation: Physis provides party HoT AND boosts healing received. At 60%, it recovers the party efficiently. Use it.

questions.scenario: Heavy raidwide coming. Party at full HP.
questions.question: How should you use Holos?
questions.explanation: Holos provides party shield (mitigation) AND heal. Use BEFORE raidwide for the shield value, then the heal tops off remaining damage.

questions.scenario: Party needs healing. Boss is targetable. Pneuma available.
questions.question: What's special about Pneuma?
questions.explanation: Pneuma deals line AoE damage AND heals the party. It's both DPS and healing - use it when the boss is targetable and party needs HP.

questions.scenario: Tank about to take heavy damage. Krasis available.
questions.question: What does Krasis do?
questions.explanation: Krasis increases healing received by 20% for the target. Apply it before healing the tank for boosted effectiveness.

questions.scenario: Physis is ready. Light damage, party at 85% HP.
questions.question: Should you use Physis?
questions.explanation: If heavy damage is coming soon, save Physis for that. If nothing major is coming, using it now for the HoT is fine since it's 60s CD.

questions.scenario: Tankbuster with multi-hit (3 hits rapidly). Haima available.
questions.question: Is Haima good here?
questions.explanation: Haima's shields refresh when broken (up to 5 times). Multi-hit busters are PERFECT for Haima - each hit triggers a new shield.

questions.scenario: Multiple raidwides over 15 seconds. Panhaima available.
questions.question: When to use Panhaima?
questions.explanation: Panhaima puts stacking shields on the party (refresh on break). Use BEFORE a multi-hit sequence to maximize shield value.

questions.scenario: Party has E.Prognosis shields. Unexpected damage requires healing.
questions.question: What does Pepsis do?
questions.explanation: Pepsis instantly converts your existing shields into healing. If shields would expire unused, Pepsis gets value from them.

questions.scenario: Tank critically low. You need a big heal. Zoe available.
questions.question: How do you use Zoe?
questions.explanation: Zoe boosts your next GCD heal by 50%. Use Zoe then Diagnosis for a massive single-target heal. Druochole is oGCD (not boosted).

questions.scenario: You have 0 Addersgall. Rhizomata available.
questions.question: What does Rhizomata provide?
questions.explanation: Rhizomata grants 1 Addersgall stack on a 90s cooldown. Use it when you need Addersgall abilities but are out of stacks.

questions.scenario: Party healthy. E.Dosis up. Kardia on tank.
questions.question: What should you be doing?
questions.explanation: Dosis spam = damage + Kardia healing. When party is healthy and DoT is up, maximize Dosis uptime. SGE heals through DPS.

questions.scenario: Burst window starting. You have 2 Phlegma charges.
questions.question: How should you use Phlegma?
questions.explanation: Phlegma is strong instant damage. Use charges during burst windows (under raid buffs) for maximum value. Save for movement only if burst is far off.

questions.scenario: Paired with WHM. They use Temperance before raidwide.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: WHM's Temperance provides party mitigation. Stacking more mitigation has diminishing returns. Trust co-healer, focus on DPS.

questions.scenario: You have 2 Addersting (from broken E.Diagnosis shields). Need to move.
questions.question: What should you use?
questions.explanation: Toxikon is instant damage using Addersting. Perfect for movement. You earned those stacks from shields - use them.

questions.scenario: DPS died. Swiftcast ready. AST co-healer casting Malefic.
questions.question: Who should raise?
questions.explanation: Your Swiftcast is ready and AST is DPSing. Take the raise - get the DPS back immediately.
